title: centaurea phrygia subsp. carpatica
intro: Centaurea phrygia subsp. carpatica, also known as the Alpine knapweed or Carpatic centaury, is a perennial plant species belonging to the daisy family Asteraceae. It is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional folk medicine, where it is used to treat various health issues such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems. The plant is rich in antioxidants, flavonoids, and other bioactive compounds, which have been found to have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, contributing to its health benefits. The herbal preparations of Centaurea phrygia subsp. carpatica can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to alleviate symptoms of colds, coughs, and other respiratory conditions.
